glossary.title: How Our Stats Work
glossary.intro: We don't hide our math. Every stat on the site is explained below — what it means and exactly how we work it out. Think something is wrong, or know a better way to measure it? We'd genuinely love to hear it.

legend.title: Notation
legend.note: Brackets narrow a stat to a subset — e.g. H (rifle) = rifle hits, S (spotted) = shots while an enemy was visible. Per-round rates (KPR, DPR, APR, ADR, KAST) reuse these letters over R.

scores.title: Reading the 0–100 ratings
scores.body: Aim, Positioning, Utility and the per-stat ratings are scored out of 100 — but it's a percentile, not a percentage. 50 is an average player; 90 means you're better than ~90% of players; 20 means most players do better. This can surprise you: almost everyone counter-strafes, and the typical player does it ~81% of the time — so counter-strafing 80% of the time actually lands around 22 (just below average), even though 80% sounds high. The rating tells you where you stand versus everyone else; the raw rate is still in the match stat tables.

performance.title: Performance ratings (Aim / Positioning / Utility)
performance.body: We take the underlying stats for each area, score each one as a percentile against every other player (using players with at least 30 rounds), then average them into a single 0–100 rating per area. Aim grows richer over time: stats like crosshair placement and time-to-damage fold in as more demos are parsed.

consistency.title: Consistency
consistency.body: Each match gets one overall score — your Aim, Positioning and Utility for that match, combined. The chart plots it over your recent matches, and the consistency number reflects how little it swings: a flatter line scores higher.

focus.title: Focus areas
focus.body: Your three lowest-scoring underlying stats — the spots with the most room to grow — each with a short tip. They update as you play.

geometry.title: Line of sight & the 3D model
geometry.body: On maps we have a collision mesh for, we load the map's 3D geometry and ray-cast between players to confirm a real line of sight. So 'spotted', crosshair placement, time-to-damage and engagement stats only count when you could actually see the enemy — not through smoke or a wall. On maps without a mesh we fall back to the game's spotted flag and label those stats 'Estimated'.

glossary.scale_legend: — rough scale of what subpar to elite looks like for each stat
glossary.scale_hint: Rough subpar → elite scale (estimated, not from your data yet)
glossary.mesh_help: Uses the map's 3D collision mesh. We ray-cast a line of sight between the two players, so only real sightlines count — spots through smoke, walls or thin gaps are filtered out. Needs the map geometry to be available; falls back to the raw engine data when it isn't.
glossary.mesh_legend: marks a stat validated against the map's 3D collision mesh (line-of-sight checked)
glossary.mesh_coverage_note: Mesh-validated stats (marked above) only get their line-of-sight check on maps we have a collision mesh for. Official maps without a mesh fall back to the raw engine spotted flag. Workshop maps are not listed here.
glossary.mesh_coverage_loading: Checking which maps have a mesh…
glossary.mesh_missing_none: Every official map has a mesh.

var_help.h: Hits — bullets that hit an enemy
var_help.theta: Angle from your aim to the enemy
var_help.sv: Survived — you lived to the end of the round
var_help.tr: Traded — your death was avenged by a teammate
var_help.n: a count — the number used in this expression
var_help.spotted: only while the enemy was visible to you
var_help.rifle: rifles only — AK, M4 and similar
var_help.head: headshots only
var_help.first_sight: the first moment the mesh confirms you could see the enemy
var_help.first_shot: the first bullet of an engagement
var_help.spray: the bullets after the first in a burst
var_help.stopped: fired while standing still — you counter-strafed first
var_help.on_target: time your crosshair was on the enemy
var_help.engaged: the full time you were in the fight
var_help.damaged: enemies you actually dealt damage to
var_help.traded: your death was avenged by a teammate moments later
var_help.trade_chance: deaths where a teammate was positioned to trade

time_to_damage.expr: avg(t from first sight to first damage)

counter_strafing.note: rifle shots at a spotted enemy

flash_assists.expr: count(assists set up by your flash)

unused_utility.expr: total nade $ still on you when you die

elo_change.note: Kf (K-factor) = 500; series scales by map differential in a Bo-N

elo_impact.expr: your KDA vs the team + damage share → 0.8–1.2
elo_impact.note: on a loss it's inverted so strong performers lose less

clutch_against.expr: enemies alive when the clutch began

role_sniper.expr: main AWPer — K(awp) ≥ 5 and ≥ 20% of K

traded_death_opportunities.expr: count(D with a teammate able to trade)

traded_death_attempts.expr: count(D a teammate shot to avenge)

multi_kill_breakdown.expr: count(rounds with exactly N K), N = 2–5

buy_matchup.expr: R won (your buy vs theirs) ÷ R (matchup) × 100

h2h_flashes.expr: count(your flashes that blind this enemy)

h2h_weapon_class.expr: your K on this enemy, by weapon class

weapon_rating.expr: blend of KPR, ADR & impact per weapon

aim.label: Aim
aim.description: Your shooting — accuracy, headshots, and how quickly/cleanly you hit visible enemies. Scored 0–100 as the average percentile of those aim stats vs all players (50 = average).

positioning.label: Positioning
positioning.description: Where you fight — surviving rounds, dying near teammates so you get traded, and contributing every round (KAST). Scored 0–100 as the average percentile of those stats vs all players (50 = average).

utility.label: Utility
utility.description: Grenade impact — flash assists, how much you blind enemies, and HE/molotov damage per nade. Scored 0–100 as the average percentile vs all players. Provisional: utility doesn't yet cleanly separate skill on our data.

consistency.label: Consistency
consistency.description: Each match gets one overall score (Aim, Positioning and Utility combined). Consistency reflects how little that score swings match to match — less swing is better.

accuracy.label: Accuracy
accuracy.description: Bullets that hit an enemy, out of every bullet you fired.

accuracy_spotted.label: Spotted Accuracy
accuracy_spotted.description: Your hit rate while an enemy was actually visible — spam through smoke or walls doesn't count.

crosshair_placement.label: Crosshair Placement
crosshair_placement.description: How close your crosshair already is to the enemy when they appear. A smaller angle means less to move before firing.

time_to_damage.label: Time to Damage
time_to_damage.description: How fast you deal damage after first seeing an enemy — a proxy for reaction speed.

counter_strafing.label: Counter-Strafing
counter_strafing.description: How often you fully stop moving before firing. Shots while moving are wildly inaccurate.

first_bullet.label: First Bullet
first_bullet.description: How often your opening shot of a duel hits — the shot that decides most fights.

tracking.label: Tracking
tracking.description: While engaging a visible enemy, how much of the time your crosshair stays on them.

flash_assists.label: Flash Assists
flash_assists.description: Kills your flash set up — you blinded an enemy and a teammate killed them shortly after.

flash_blind.label: Flash Effectiveness
flash_blind.description: How much enemy blindness your flashes actually cause — rewards pop-flashes, not flashes thrown over heads.

util_efficiency.label: Utility Damage
util_efficiency.description: Damage dealt per HE/molotov you throw — rewards landing nades, not just buying them.

trade_kills.label: Trade Kills
trade_kills.description: Kills where you avenged a teammate who had just died (within a few seconds).

traded.label: Traded Deaths
traded.description: How often, when you die, a teammate trades the kill back right after — a sign of fighting near your team.

opening.label: Opening Duels
opening.description: Your win rate in the first duel of the round — first kill versus first death.

opening_attempts.label: Opening Attempts
opening_attempts.description: How often you take the round's first duel.

buy_pistol.label: Pistol Round
buy_pistol.description: The first round of each half — everyone starts with only a pistol and limited cash.

buy_eco.label: Eco
buy_eco.description: An 'eco' round: the team deliberately saves, buying little or nothing (under $5,000 total).

buy_force.label: Force Buy
buy_force.description: A force / half buy: the team spends most of its money but can't afford a full setup ($5,000–$20,000).

buy_full.label: Full Buy
buy_full.description: A full buy: rifles, armour and utility for the whole team (over $20,000).

buy_win_rate.label: Buy Win %
buy_win_rate.description: How often the team wins rounds on a given buy type — useful for spotting weak eco or anti-eco rounds.

man_advantage.label: Man Advantage %
man_advantage.description: How often you win the round after winning the opening duel — closing out the rounds you start ahead in.

man_disadvantage.label: Man Disadvantage %
man_disadvantage.description: How often you still win the round after losing the opening duel — clawing back rounds you start behind in.

net_trade.label: Net Trade
net_trade.description: Your trade kills minus your own deaths that a teammate failed to trade back — positive means you trade more than you cost the team.

trade_kill_opportunities.label: Trade Kill Opps
trade_kill_opportunities.description: How often a teammate died near you, giving you a chance to trade the killer.

trade_kill_attempts.label: Trade Kill Attempts
trade_kill_attempts.description: Of those chances, how often you actually shot at the killer.

traded_death_opportunities.label: Traded Death Opps
traded_death_opportunities.description: Your deaths where a teammate was positioned to trade you back.

traded_death_attempts.label: Traded Death Attempts
traded_death_attempts.description: Of those, how often a teammate actually shot at your killer.

traded_death_pct.label: Traded Death %
traded_death_pct.description: Share of your deaths that a teammate traded back — the same idea as Traded Deaths, shown as its own column.

total_damage.label: Total Damage
total_damage.description: Total damage you dealt to enemies across the map (ADR is this per round).

team_damage.label: Team Damage
team_damage.description: Damage you dealt to teammates (friendly fire) — lower is better.

zeus_kills.label: Zeus Kills
zeus_kills.description: Kills with the Zeus x27 taser.

multi_kill_breakdown.label: Multi-Kill Rounds (2K–5K)
multi_kill_breakdown.description: How many rounds you got exactly 2, 3, 4 or 5 kills in — the breakdown behind Multi-Kill Rounds.

flashes_thrown.label: Flashes Thrown
flashes_thrown.description: How many flashbangs you threw, effective or not.

smokes_thrown.label: Smokes Thrown
smokes_thrown.description: How many smoke grenades you threw.

round_win_pct.label: Round Win %
round_win_pct.description: Share of rounds the team won.

pistol_win_pct.label: Pistol Win %
pistol_win_pct.description: Win rate on pistol rounds — the first round of each half.

opening_win_pct.label: Opening Win % (team)
opening_win_pct.description: Team win rate in the round's first duel.

team_trade_pct.label: Trade % (team)
team_trade_pct.description: How often the team converts its trade chances into trade kills.

buy_matchup.label: Buy Matchup Win %
buy_matchup.description: Round win rate for each buy-type matchup (e.g. full-buy vs eco), not just your own buy type.

h2h_kills.label: H2H Kills
h2h_kills.description: Kills you got on a specific opponent in this match.

h2h_damage.label: H2H Damage
h2h_damage.description: Damage you dealt to a specific opponent in this match.

h2h_flashes.label: H2H Flashes
h2h_flashes.description: How many times your flashes blinded a specific opponent.

h2h_weapon_class.label: H2H by Weapon
h2h_weapon_class.description: Your kills on an opponent grouped by weapon class — rifle, pistol, sniper, and so on.

weapon_usage_pct.label: Most Used %
weapon_usage_pct.description: Share of all your kills that came with this weapon.

weapon_rating.label: Weapon Rating
weapon_rating.description: Approximate HLTV-style rating for a single weapon from its KPR, ADR and impact — directional, since there are no per-weapon deaths or KAST.

weapon_economy.label: Weapon Economy
weapon_economy.description: A weapon's rating adjusted for its buy cost — about 1.0 means it performs on par for the price, higher means great value.

trade_kill_pct.label: Trade Kill %
trade_kill_pct.description: Share of your trade chances you convert — when a teammate dies near you, how often you get the avenging kill.

he_damage.label: HE Damage
he_damage.description: Damage your HE grenades deal to enemies.

molotov_damage.label: Molotov Damage
molotov_damage.description: Damage your molotovs and incendiaries deal to enemies.

he_team_damage.label: HE Team Damage
he_team_damage.description: Damage your HE grenades deal to teammates — lower is better.

enemies_flashed.label: Enemies Flashed
enemies_flashed.description: Number of enemies your flashes blinded for a meaningful amount of time (over one second).

team_flashed.label: Teammates Flashed
team_flashed.description: Number of teammates your flashes blinded — lower is better.

avg_flash_duration.label: Avg Flash Duration
avg_flash_duration.description: Average time an enemy stays blinded per flash that hits — longer pop-flashes score higher.

unused_utility.label: Unused Utility
unused_utility.description: The dollar value of grenades you were still holding when you died — utility you bought but never used.

wasted_magazine_pct.label: Wasted Mag %
wasted_magazine_pct.description: Share of your magazine left unfired when you reload — high values mean reloading too early.

elo_change.label: ELO Change
elo_change.description: How much your ELO moved after a match — scaled by the result, your performance and the series length.

elo_impact.label: ELO Impact
elo_impact.description: A 0.8–1.2 multiplier on your ELO change from how you performed relative to your team (KDA and damage share).

clutch.label: Clutch
clutch.description: A 1vN situation — you're the last player alive on your team against one or more standing enemies.

clutch_against.label: Against (N)
clutch_against.description: How many enemies were alive when your clutch began — the N in 1vN.

clutch_won.label: Clutch Won
clutch_won.description: You won the clutch by eliminating every remaining enemy yourself.

clutch_saved.label: Clutch Saved
clutch_saved.description: Your team won the round while you were still alive, without you having to kill everyone.

clutch_lost.label: Clutch Lost
clutch_lost.description: You died before the round was won.

clutch_win_rate.label: Clutch Win %
clutch_win_rate.description: Share of your clutches that ended in a win (saves and losses count in the denominator).

kills_in_clutch.label: Clutch Kills
kills_in_clutch.description: Kills you made while in a clutch situation.

role_sniper.label: Sniper (role)
role_sniper.description: The team's primary AWPer on the map — at least 5 AWP kills and 20%+ of their kills with the AWP.

role_entry.label: Entry (role)
role_entry.description: The player who takes the round's first duel most often (the AWPer aside).

role_support.label: Support (role)
role_support.description: The player contributing the most utility value (the AWPer and entry aside).

role_rifler.label: Rifler (role)
role_rifler.description: The default role — a rifler who isn't the AWPer, entry, or main support.

awp_share.label: AWP Share
awp_share.description: The share of your kills that came with the AWP or scoped rifle — drives the Sniper role.

entry_rate.label: Entry Rate
entry_rate.description: How often you take the round's first duel — drives the Entry role.

support_idx.label: Support Index
support_idx.description: A utility score combining flash assists, grenade damage and flashes thrown, per round — drives the Support role.

enemies_flashed_per.label: Enemies / Flash
enemies_flashed_per.description: Average number of enemies blinded per flash thrown — higher means more effective flashes.

opening_attempts_pct.label: Attempt Rate
opening_attempts_pct.description: Share of rounds where this player took the opening duel (first engagement of the round).

opening_success_pct.label: Win Rate
opening_success_pct.description: Share of opening duels this player won.

opening_traded_pct.label: Traded Rate
opening_traded_pct.description: Share of opening deaths that were traded back by a teammate.

survived_pct.label: Survival Rate
survived_pct.description: Share of rounds this player survived (lived to the end of the round).

hltv.label: HLTV Rating
hltv.description: HLTV 2.0 overall performance rating combining kills, deaths, KAST, damage and impact. ~1.0 is average.

rating.label: HLTV Rating
rating.description: HLTV 2.0 overall performance rating combining kills, deaths, KAST, damage and impact. ~1.0 is average.

adr.label: ADR
adr.description: Average Damage per Round — how much damage you deal each round.

kast.label: KAST
kast.description: Percentage of rounds where you got a Kill, Assist, Survived, or were Traded.

udr.label: UDR
udr.description: Utility Damage per Round — grenade (HE + molotov) damage each round.

hs.label: Headshot %
hs.description: Share of your kills that were headshots.

far.label: FAR
far.description: First-Action Rating — performance in opening (first kill of the round) duels.

elo.label: ELO
elo.description: 5Stack matchmaking skill rating — rises and falls with wins and losses.

kda.label: K / D / A
kda.description: Average Kills / Deaths / Assists per match.

a.label: Assists
a.description: Total assists — you damaged or flashed an enemy a teammate then killed.

kd.label: K/D
kd.description: Kill/Death ratio — kills divided by deaths. Above 1.0 means more kills than deaths.

mkr.label: Multi-Kill Rounds
mkr.description: Rounds in which you got two or more kills.

mp.label: Maps Played
mp.description: Number of maps this player has played in the stage.

cl.label: Clutch %
cl.description: Share of clutch situations (last player alive, 1vX) that you won.

cast.label: CAST
cast.description: Clutch attempts — the number of clutch (1vX) situations you were put in.

awp.label: AWP
awp.description: AWP usage / sniper role signal — how much a player relies on the AWP.

discord.webhook_description: We use this discord webhook to send notifications that require action from a player. Ex. match issues, bugs, etc.
discord.support_role_description: These roles will be pinged when used for support actions. You can add multiple Role IDs separated by commas. Get a Role ID from Discord by enabling developer mode, then right-clicking the role and selecting Copy ID.

match_notifications.title: Match Status Notifications
match_notifications.description: Configure Discord notifications for match status changes. These settings control both panel and Discord notifications.
match_notifications.webhook_description: Dedicated webhook for match status notifications. Falls back to the default webhook if not set.
match_notifications.role_id_description: These roles will be mentioned in match status notifications. You can add multiple Role IDs separated by commas.

gamedata_notifications.title: Gamedata Validation Notifications
gamedata_notifications.description: Configure where CS2 gamedata signature validation results are posted.
gamedata_notifications.webhook_description: Dedicated webhook for gamedata validation results. Falls back to the default webhook if not set.
gamedata_notifications.role_id_description: These roles will be mentioned in gamedata validation notifications. You can add multiple Role IDs separated by commas. Falls back to the default role if not set.

server_notifications.title: Server Notifications
server_notifications.description: Configure Discord notifications for game server nodes.
server_notifications.disk_warning_percent_description: Send a deletable notification when disk usage exceeds this percentage. Set to 0 to disable.
server_notifications.disk_critical_percent_description: Send a non-deletable notification when disk usage exceeds this percentage. Set to 0 to disable.

streaming.title: Streaming
streaming.minimum_role_to_stream: Minimum Role Allowed to Connect to TV (Delayed)
streaming.minimum_role_to_stream_description: GOTV broadcast — viewers connect to the delayed TV port (broadcast delay applies). Safe for casters and audiences.
streaming.minimum_role_to_spectate: Minimum Role Allowed to Spectate (No Delay)
streaming.minimum_role_to_spectate_description: Real-time spectator — connects directly to the game server with no delay. Restrict to trusted roles to prevent ghosting.
streaming.require_login_section: Require Sign-In to View Live Streams
streaming.require_login_description: Hide live game streams from signed-out visitors. Discourages cheating by ensuring every viewer is an identifiable account.
streaming.playcast_description: Enable Playcast over using TV ports directly.
streaming.live_video_codec_description: H.264 is the default for the broadest browser and device compatibility, including the lowest-latency WebRTC path on every browser. H.265 (HEVC) is ~30% smaller at matched quality, but HEVC-over-WebRTC is Safari 17+ only — Chrome and Firefox viewers fall back to the HLS path. H.265 falls back to H.264 automatically if the GPU lacks NVENC HEVC.

application.create_matches_role: Minimum Role Allowed to Create Matches
application.create_tournaments_role: Minimum Role Allowed to Create Tournaments
application.dedicated_servers_min_role_to_connect: Minimum Role to Connect to Dedicated Password-Protected Servers
application.matchmaking_min_role: Minimum Role Allowed to Join Matchmaking

draft_add_without_invite.title: Draft Lobby Invites
draft_add_without_invite.description: Controls who can add players directly to a draft lobby versus sending an invite the player must accept.
draft_add_without_invite.label: Minimum Role to Add Players to Lineup Without Invite

application.max_acceptable_latency_description: Players above this ping to a server are filtered out when picking the best server for a match.
application.auto_cancel_duration_description: Cancels a match that hasn't gone live (still in picking / veto / waiting) after this many minutes.
application.live_match_timeout_description: Safety timeout — a live match is auto-cancelled after this many minutes if it never finishes.
application.matchmaking_type_description: Toggle matchmaking for each type that will allow players to join matchmaking for this type of match.

players.force_name_registration_description: If forced to have player registration, the player will be required to submit a name that will be used on the platform.
players.refresh_all_description: Re-sync all player data (name, ELO, stats, sanctions) into the search index. Use this if a player looks outdated or missing in search but their profile is correct.
players.refresh_dialog_description: This will re-sync every player into the Typesense search index in batches. It runs in the background and you can watch its progress here.
players.recompute_elo_description: Wipe and rebuild every player's ELO by replaying all finished matches in order, in batches. When seasons are enabled this also rebuilds each season's ELO from the matches that fall within it. Use this after an ELO formula change or if ratings look wrong. Search results update automatically as ELO is recalculated.
players.recompute_elo_dialog_description: This wipes all existing ELO and rebuilds it from scratch by replaying every finished match in chronological order (in batches). The leaderboard will be temporarily incomplete while it runs. You can watch its progress here.
players.scan_bans_description: Check every player who has been on a lineup for Steam VAC/game bans. Accounts with an active ban are auto-banned.
players.scan_failed: Failed to queue Steam ban scan
players.scan_dialog_title: Scan all players for Steam bans?
players.scan_dialog_description: This queues a background Steam VAC/game-ban check for every player who has been on a lineup. Detected bans are applied automatically; this may take a while depending on the number of players.
players.danger_zone_description: These actions are resource-intensive and run in the background across every player. They run automatically when needed — only trigger them manually if you really need to.

branding.brand_name_description: Displayed in the sidebar and page title.
branding.border_radius_description: Controls the roundness of buttons, cards, and inputs.
branding.show_separators_description: Display horizontal divider lines between sections.
branding.logo_description: Upload one square image (512×512 PNG recommended). Used app-wide and to generate your PWA install icon. ICO files aren't supported.
branding.favicon_description: Optional. A simpler mark for the browser tab. PNG, ICO or WebP.
branding.login_page_description: Customize the login page footer link.
branding.show_login_footer_description: Display a footer link on the login page.
branding.theme_colors_description: Customize the theme colors. Changes are previewed live.
branding.reset_confirm_description: This clears your brand name, colors, login footer, and uploaded logo/favicon/app icon, restoring the default 5Stack theme. This cannot be undone.
branding.assets_section_description: Upload a logo (used app-wide and to generate your PWA install icon) and an optional favicon.

fixtures.dev_only_notice: Fixtures are only available in development mode.
fixtures.load_description: Populate the database with sample players, teams, matches, and tournaments for testing.
fixtures.loaded_description: Fixture data is currently loaded in the database.
fixtures.load_dialog_description: This will insert 40 players, 8 teams, ~120 matches, and 4 tournaments into the database. This is sample data for development purposes.
fixtures.reload_dialog_description: This will delete all existing fixture data and reload fresh copies. Any changes you've made to fixture players, matches, or tournaments will be lost.
fixtures.remove_dialog_description: This will delete all fixture players, teams, matches, and tournaments from the database. This action cannot be undone.

servers.title: Server Settings
servers.plugin_runtime_description: Which CS2 plugin framework your game servers run. Both frameworks implement the same match logic, but they load different third-party plugins and gamedata files.
servers.plugin_runtime_warning: Switching does not move your custom plugins between addons/swiftlys2 and addons/counterstrikesharp. Servers already running keep their current image until they are recycled.
servers.plugin_runtime_locked: The SERVER_IMAGE environment variable is set on the panel API and overrides this choice. Remove it to select a runtime here.
servers.enable_cpu_pinning_description: Enable CPU pinning on game server nodes for better performance
servers.number_of_cpus_per_server_description: Number of dedicated CPU cores assigned to each game server (only used when CPU pinning is enabled).
servers.reserved_disk_space_fresh_gb_description: Minimum disk space in GB required for a fresh node provisioning (CS2 not yet installed). Set to 0 to disable.
servers.reserved_disk_space_existing_gb_descript: Minimum disk space in GB required when CS2 is already installed on the node. Set to 0 to disable.

release_channel.title: Release Channel
release_channel.description: Choose which release channel this install tracks. Latest is the stable channel; Beta receives pre-release builds early.
release_channel.beta_warning: Beta is a pre-release channel and may be unstable. Services will be updated to pre-release builds as they become available.
release_channel.fell_back: beta not available yet — using latest

external_matches.title: Match Imports
external_matches.enabled_description: Let players link their Steam match-history auth code to import official CS2 matches, and allow demo uploads. Turn this off to hide the auth-code form and block external imports.
external_matches.disabled_notice: External match importing is currently disabled by your administrator.
external_matches.faceit_enabled_description: Off by default. Poll linked players' Faceit match history (last 30 days) and import their matches. Requires External Matches enabled and a Faceit API key — demo download also needs Downloads API access.
external_matches.faceit_test_description: Verify the Faceit API key works and whether it can download demos.

steam_presence.title: Steam Presence
steam_presence.enabled_description: On by default. Friend bots watch linked players' live Steam presence and import their matches the moment they finish, instead of waiting for the hourly poll. Turn off as a kill-switch.
steam_presence.setup_step1: Dedicate a Steam account for the bot with Steam Guard enabled. Don't reuse your STEAM_USER account — that one is used elsewhere and would conflict.
steam_presence.setup_step2: Add it below. It logs in automatically; if Steam Guard prompts, a 2FA box appears on the bot — enter the code from email or the Steam app. After the first login it stays signed in and won't ask again.
steam_presence.setup_step3: Players add the bot as a Steam friend (from their settings or the profile link). Once friends, their matches import the moment they finish.
steam_presence.add_bot_description: Add a Steam account to the presence pool. Its credentials are stored to keep it signed in.
steam_presence.account_added: Bot account added — it will log in shortly
steam_presence.remove_confirm: Remove bot account {name}? Players assigned to it will be reassigned.
steam_presence.needs_2fa_email: Enter the Steam Guard code emailed to this account to finish login.
steam_presence.needs_2fa_app: Enter the Steam Guard code from the Steam mobile app for this account to finish login.
steam_presence.code_wrong: Previous code was incorrect — try again.
steam_presence.code_failed: Failed to submit Steam Guard code
steam_presence.bots_description: Steam accounts in the presence pool and their live status.
steam_presence.no_bots_hint: Add a Steam account to start watching players' matches.
steam_presence.pending_tooltip: Players who were assigned a bot but haven't added it as a Steam friend yet.
steam_presence.capacity_tooltip: Total friend slots across all bots. Auto-detected from each bot's Steam level (250 + 5 × level, max 2000).
steam_presence.fetch_hint: Make sure the API is running the latest build, migrations and Hasura metadata are applied, and the web GraphQL types are regenerated (yarn codegen).

scrim_finder.title: Scrim Finder
scrim_finder.enabled_description: Let teams advertise availability, find opponents, and schedule scrims. Enabled by default. Turn this off to hide the Scrim Finder everywhere and stop auto-matching.

demo_settings.title: Demos
demo_settings.retention_storage_description: Demos are kept at least the minimum retention period. Once total storage exceeds the maximum, the oldest demos past their retention are deleted first.
demo_settings.cloudflare_worker_url_description: Optional Cloudflare Worker that proxies requests to your S3 bucket so demo + highlight downloads don't count against your bucket's egress (free Backblaze ↔ Cloudflare bandwidth). Leave blank to serve directly from S3.
demo_settings.test_s3_description: Test the S3 connection and upload a file to the demo bucket.
demo_settings.demo_network_limiter_description: Caps the upload bandwidth a game-server node will use when transferring finished demo files to S3.
demo_settings.clips_retention_storage_description: Highlights are kept at least the minimum retention period. Once total storage exceeds the maximum, the oldest highlights past their retention are deleted first.
demo_settings.clip_video_codec_description: H.264 is the default for the broadest playback compatibility across browsers and devices. H.265 (HEVC) produces ~30% smaller files at matched visual quality and is decoded in hardware by modern Chrome, Edge, Safari, and iOS, but is less universally supported. H.265 falls back to H.264 automatically if the GPU lacks NVENC HEVC.
demo_settings.clip_fps_description: Output frame rate for rendered highlights and manually-edited clips. 60 FPS is smoother but doubles the encode work and file size; 30 FPS is the lighter option.
demo_settings.clip_resolution_description: Default output resolution for rendered highlights and manually-edited clips. Users can still override per-clip in the render dialog. 1080p balances quality and file size; 720p produces ~50% smaller files. Both benefit when GPU nodes render at 1440p — the streamer supersamples that down to the selected output resolution.
demo_settings.clip_bake_branding: Bake player chip + outro into highlights
demo_settings.clip_bake_branding_description: Renders the player chip overlay and brand outro directly into the highlight file. Disable to ship raw captures without overlays.
demo_settings.auto_generate_match_clips_description: Queue a match-recap highlight for each player after their match finishes.
demo_settings.auto_generate_match_clips_imported_descr: Also auto-generate highlights for matches imported from outside 5stack (e.g. Valve match history). Off by default so imported matches don't flood the render queue.
demo_settings.pause_renders_during_active_match: Pause Highlight Renders During Active Match
demo_settings.pause_renders_during_active_match_descri: When a match goes Live on a server backed by a GPU node, auto-pause in-flight highlight renders so the game server isn't competing with the render pod for GPU. Renders resume automatically once the match ends.
demo_settings.auto_clip_always_include_knife_descripti: Append up to 3 knife kills to the end of a player's highlight reel when they got a knife kill — even when their round doesn't meet the minimum-kills filter.
demo_settings.auto_clip_min_kills: Minimum kills to generate a highlight
demo_settings.auto_clip_min_kills_description: Only auto-generate a player's highlight when their best round reached this many kills. Raise it so a match doesn't spawn a clip for everyone.
demo_settings.default_hud_mode_description: Which JTs Hud Manager layout the game-streamer pod loads at boot for live streams, demo playback, and batch highlights. Streamers can still switch styles per-stream from the player controls.
demo_settings.reparse_warning_title: This reparses every demo — it can take a very long time.
demo_settings.reparse_warning_body: Demos are reprocessed one at a time, which can run for hours or days depending on how many you have. Only do this if you know what you're doing, or after a major update changes how demos are parsed. New stats from updates are reparsed automatically as matches are viewed — this is only to force everything at once.
demo_settings.reparse_confirm_body: This queues a background job that reparses all demos one at a time and can take a very long time. It's not recommended unless you know what you're doing or a major update changed demo parsing. You can cancel it at any point.
demo_settings.reparse_started: Reparse started — it runs in the background, one demo at a time. You can close this page.
demo_settings.reparse_cancel_requested: Cancel requested — stopping after the current demo.
demo_settings.orphaned_dialog_description: Scans the bucket for demo, playback and highlight objects with no matching database row, and corrects the tracked storage sizes. Runs in the background — you can close this dialog and check back.
demo_settings.orphaned_scanning: Scanning… this can take a while. You can safely close this and check back later.
demo_settings.orphaned_no_results: No scan has been run yet.
demo_settings.orphaned_delete_confirm: Permanently delete {count} orphaned object(s) ({size})? This cannot be undone.
demo_settings.orphaned_scan_started: Scan started — this can take a while depending on how many objects you have. You'll get a notification when it's done, so you can close this.
demo_settings.orphaned_tooltip: Find demo, playback and highlight files in S3 that are no longer referenced in the database, and reclaim the space. Runs in the background.

update_map_pools.title: Automatic Update Map Pools
update_map_pools.description: Automatically add / update map pools when new maps are added or removed from the active duty pool.

leagues.title: League
leagues.description: Seasonal round-robin play across skill divisions with weekly match windows, roster locks, playoffs and promotion/relegation between seasons. Managed by administrators.
leagues.manage_description: Create and manage seasons, registrations and schedules from the league page.
leagues.divisions_description: The tiers teams are placed into and promoted/relegated between each season. A tier with no teams simply does not run that season.
leagues.allow_request_hint: When off, teams register with no division preference and admins place them.
leagues.registration_description: How teams register for league seasons

teams.roster_description: Team-wide roster rules used across leagues and tournaments: how many players a roster holds, and how many substitutes back up the five starters.
teams.roster_hint: Teams need at least the minimum to be approved and can't exceed the maximum.

matchmaking.max_latency_description: If no regions are preferred, matchmaking will only use regions with latency below this value.

show_match_ready_modal.title: Match & Draft Room Popup
show_match_ready_modal.description: Show a popup when your match or draft room is ready, with a quick link to jump to it. When disabled, you won't be redirected — reach it from the button in the top nav instead.

external_matches.presence_description: Add the bot as a Steam friend to auto-import your Competitive & Premier matches the moment they end.

external_matches.unlink_confirm_description: We'll stop importing your official CS2 matches and Premier rank. You can re-link anytime with your Steam auth and share codes.
external_matches.polling_banner_description: Scanning Valve for your recent matches. Pending imports will appear below as we find them.
external_matches.pending_imports_description: Matches your linked accounts are waiting on. Demos that fail here are typically older than 30 days and no longer hosted by Valve.
external_matches.upload_demo_description: Drop in a CS2 .dem file to import the match stats. Works whether or not your match history is linked.
external_matches.click_to_choose: Click to choose or drag a .dem file
external_matches.demo_files_only: CS2 demo files only · single file

seasons.title: Seasons

seasons.rebuild_confirm_description: Recomputes this season's ELO and stats from all of its matches. This normally runs automatically — only rebuild if the data looks wrong. It runs in the background and may take a while.

gpu_nodes.title: GPU Nodes
gpu_nodes.description: GPU-capable game server nodes that host live streams, demo playback, and highlight renders. Each GPU runs at most one consumer at a time.
gpu_nodes.toggle_enabled_help: When off, the node is fully disabled — no GPU work and no match scheduling.
gpu_nodes.toggle_scheduling_help: When off, the node keeps running existing matches but won't accept new ones.

steam_pool.delete_description: Any running pod using this account keeps its credentials and finishes normally. The account will be removed from the pool and not available for future jobs.

stats.estimated_tooltip: This map has no collision mesh, so line-of-sight couldn't be validated — spotted, crosshair, time-to-damage and engagement stats here are estimated.

accuracy.title: Accuracy (All Shots)
accuracy.description: Out of the total bullets you fired, how many hit an enemy. Volume-dependent — snipers and pistol players fire rarely and hit a high fraction, so this number flatters them. Compare the per-weapon accuracies instead.
accuracy.calculation: All hits divided by all shots, across every weapon.

rifle_accuracy.title: Rifle Accuracy
rifle_accuracy.description: Accuracy with rifles only (AK, M4, AUG, Galil, FAMAS, SG). The fairest aim comparison — rifle vs rifle — since it isn't inflated by low-fire-rate weapons.
rifle_accuracy.calculation: Rifle hits divided by rifle shots. Shown only with at least 5 rifle shots.

pistol_accuracy.title: Pistol Accuracy
pistol_accuracy.description: Accuracy with pistols only (Glock, USP, P250, Deagle, Five-Seven, Tec-9, CZ, Dualies, Revolver).
pistol_accuracy.calculation: Pistol hits divided by pistol shots. Shown only with at least 5 pistol shots.

sniper_accuracy.title: Sniper Accuracy
sniper_accuracy.description: Accuracy with scoped rifles (AWP, SSG, SCAR-20, G3SG1). Naturally high — these are low-volume, one-shot weapons — so judge AWPers against each other.
sniper_accuracy.calculation: Sniper hits divided by sniper shots. Shown only with at least 5 sniper shots.

accuracy_spotted.title: Accuracy (Enemy Spotted)
accuracy_spotted.description: Accuracy when you were actually shooting at an enemy in your field of view. Spam through smoke or walls is excluded.
accuracy_spotted.calculation: Hits on spotted enemies divided by shots fired while an enemy was in vision.

head_accuracy.title: Headshot Accuracy
head_accuracy.description: How many of your hits landed in the head. Excludes shots with AWP.
head_accuracy.calculation: Head hits divided by total hits (AWP excluded from both).

hs_kill_pct.title: HS Kill %
hs_kill_pct.description: Share of your kills that were headshots, as the scoreboard shows.
hs_kill_pct.calculation: Headshot kills divided by total kills.

time_to_damage.title: Time to Damage
time_to_damage.description: Average time from first seeing an enemy to dealing damage to them. Trigger-discipline / hold-angle plays are excluded.
time_to_damage.calculation: Per engagement, time from the rising-edge spot to the first damage on that enemy. Averaged across all engagements; capped at 3s.

spotted_acc.title: Spot Conversion
spotted_acc.description: Of the enemies you spotted, how many you then dealt damage to — an engagement/aggression measure, not aim. (Contrast with Accuracy (Spotted), which is your bullet hit-rate while shooting a visible enemy.)
spotted_acc.calculation: Enemies you spotted and then damaged that round, divided by the total enemies you spotted.

crosshair_placement.title: Crosshair Placement
crosshair_placement.description: Average angle (degrees) your crosshair moved from first seeing the enemy until you hit them. Lower is better.
crosshair_placement.calculation: Per engagement, the angle between your eye direction at the moment of first spot and at the first hit. Outliers above 45° are excluded.

counter_strafing.title: Counter-Strafing
counter_strafing.description: How often you stopped before firing with a rifle. Only counts rifle shots at an enemy in vision; excludes movement while fully crouched.
counter_strafing.calculation: Rifle shots at a spotted enemy where speed was under 34% of the weapon's max, divided by all rifle shots at spotted enemies (not crouched).

first_bullet_accuracy.title: First Bullet Accuracy
first_bullet_accuracy.description: How often your opening shot of an engagement connects — the shot that decides most duels. Higher is better.
first_bullet_accuracy.calculation: First shots that dealt damage divided by first shots fired, across every engagement (one per enemy you opened up on).

tracking.title: Tracking (Time on Target)
tracking.description: While engaging a live enemy, how much of the time your crosshair stayed on them. Higher is better.
tracking.calculation: Frames where your view stayed within ~5° of the enemy, divided by all frames of the engagement window.

spray_accuracy.title: Spray Accuracy
spray_accuracy.description: Your accuracy on the second and later shots of a burst — the shots where recoil matters most.
spray_accuracy.calculation: Hits attributed to spray shots divided by total spray shots. A shot is a spray shot if the same shooter fired within 250ms before it.

wasted_magazine_pct.title: Wasted Magazine %
wasted_magazine_pct.description: How often you reload with bullets still in the magazine. Lower is better — finishing the clip is more efficient.
wasted_magazine_pct.calculation: Bullets left in the mag at each reload, divided by (shots fired + wasted bullets).

trade_kill_opportunities.title: Trade Kill Opportunities
trade_kill_opportunities.description: Rounds where a teammate died while you were alive — i.e. you had a chance to trade.
trade_kill_opportunities.calculation: Distinct rounds in which at least one teammate was killed by an enemy while you were still alive.

trade_kill_attempts.title: Trade Kill Attempts
trade_kill_attempts.description: Enemies you killed shortly after they killed a teammate.
trade_kill_attempts.calculation: Kills against an enemy within 5 seconds of them killing one of your teammates.

trade_kill_pct.title: Trade Kill %
trade_kill_pct.description: How often you converted a trade opportunity into a trade kill.
trade_kill_pct.calculation: Trade kill successes divided by trade kill opportunity rounds.

traded_death_opportunities.title: Traded Death Opportunities
traded_death_opportunities.description: Your enemy deaths — i.e. each death was a chance for a teammate to trade for you.
traded_death_opportunities.calculation: Total times you were killed by an enemy.

traded_death_attempts.title: Traded Death Attempts
traded_death_attempts.description: Total trade attempts your team made on your deaths (a teammate killing your killer within 5s).
traded_death_attempts.calculation: Sum of every teammate kill that traded one of your deaths within 5 seconds.

traded_death_pct.title: Traded Death %
traded_death_pct.description: How often your deaths were avenged by a teammate.
traded_death_pct.calculation: Your deaths a teammate traded within 5 seconds, divided by total deaths.

net_trade.title: Net Trade
net_trade.description: Your contribution to your team's trade balance.
net_trade.calculation: Trade kills you completed minus your untraded deaths.

consistency.title: Consistency
consistency.description: Each match gets one overall score (your Aim, Positioning and Utility for that match, combined). This tracks it over your recent matches — a flatter line means more consistent play.
consistency.not_enough: Play a few more matches to see your form trend.

accuracy_score.label: Accuracy
accuracy_score.coach: Fewer, more deliberate shots land more often than spraying — slow down and commit to each shot.

hs_score.label: Headshots
hs_score.coach: Keep your crosshair at head level as you hold and clear angles so your first shot is a headshot.

spotted_score.label: Spotted Accuracy
spotted_score.coach: Your hit-rate when an enemy is actually visible — pre-aim the spots enemies appear from.

crosshair_score.label: Crosshair Placement
crosshair_score.coach: Hold head-level pre-aim spots so you barely have to move to land the shot.

ttd_score.label: Reaction
ttd_score.coach: Hold tighter angles — the less screen an enemy can appear in, the faster you react and fire.

counter_strafe_score.label: Counter-Strafing
counter_strafe_score.coach: Tap the opposite key to stop dead before firing — shots while moving are wildly inaccurate.

survival_score.label: Survival
survival_score.coach: Trade your life for value — avoid dry-peeking and over-extending away from your team.

traded_score.label: Trade Positioning
traded_score.coach: Fight near teammates so when you die it gets traded, instead of dying for free.

kast_score.label: Round Impact
kast_score.coach: Do something every round — a kill, assist, survive, or get traded. Empty rounds hurt your team.

flash_assists_score.label: Flash Assists
flash_assists_score.coach: Pop-flash for teammates pushing an angle, not just for yourself.

blind_score.label: Flash Effectiveness
blind_score.coach: Throw flashes that actually blind enemies right before a peek — not over their heads.

util_eff_score.label: Utility Damage
util_eff_score.coach: Learn a few HE / molotov line-ups for common spots to get real damage out of each nade.

cs2_options.title: Video Settings
cs2_options.description: Per-node video settings applied when this node hosts a live stream, demo playback, or batch highlight job.
cs2_options.section_video_description: Tune the rendering quality used while CS2 is captured on this node.

preset.title: Quality preset
preset.description: Auto lets CS2 generate the video config on first launch against the node's actual GPU. The Low / Medium / High presets apply explicit overrides on top of that.
preset.auto_active: Auto mode — CS2 will generate its own video config on first launch. The fields below are ignored until you pick another preset.

video.resolution_desc: Resolution CS2 renders at on this node. Capture always downsamples to 1080p before encoding — picking 1440p supersamples (renders 1440p → encodes 1080p), trading more GPU for sharper edges and cleaner anti-aliasing in the final stream/clip. Viewers see 1080p either way.

aa.label: Anti-aliasing
aa.description: MSAA renders multiple samples per pixel for clean geometry edges; CMAA2 is a cheaper post-process that softens edges but blurs the image. They're mutually exclusive.

video.shaderquality_desc: 0 = flatter and faster, 1 = complex highlights and reflections.
video.videocfg_shadow_quality_desc: Overall shadow detail. Higher tiers improve tactical visibility at the expense of FPS.
video.videocfg_dynamic_shadows_desc: Shadows cast by sun and players. Off saves FPS.
video.videocfg_texture_detail_desc: Texture resolution. Minimal FPS impact with enough VRAM.
video.videocfg_particle_detail_desc: Affects smoke, fire, and Molotov rendering. High improves molly visibility, lowers FPS.
video.videocfg_ao_detail_desc: Soft contact shadows. Competitive setups usually run this off.

about.title: How the league works
about.register: Register your team while signups are open and pick the players on your season roster.
about.placement: Admins review registrations and place teams into skill divisions. Returning teams keep their earned division.
about.weekly: Each week you get one matchup. Either captain proposes a time inside the match week and the opposing captain accepts — you can renegotiate until the match goes live.
about.default_night: If no time is agreed, the match defaults to the league's match night for that week.
about.roster_lock: Rosters lock partway through the season — only rostered players can play league matches, and a player can only be on one team per season.
about.playoffs: The top teams from the standings qualify for the playoff bracket; captains schedule playoff matches the same way.
about.movement: Final standings decide promotion and relegation between divisions for next season.

confirm.cancel_season_description: Cancelling ends the season for every team: registrations close, all remaining matches are called off, and the season cannot be restarted.
confirm.withdraw_description: Your team gives up its spot in this season. If registration is still open you can register again, but your place and division assignment are not held.
